expectations.text: Identifies that full-width text is too wide and recommends a maximum line length (around 45-75 characters or ~65ch)
expectations.evidence: 'Line Length Control (Max-Width)...A full-width container...will produce lines well beyond 75 characters...max-width: 65ch is an excellent default...The canonical target is 45–75 characters per line'

expectations.text: Identifies that 1.2 line-height is too tight for body text and recommends a higher value (around 1.5-1.6)
expectations.evidence: 'Your 1.2 line-height is the correct value for large display and headline text, not body text...For a normal column width, use line-height: 1.5 or line-height: 1.6'

expectations.text: Explains that line-height should be proportional to line length (wider lines need more line-height)
expectations.evidence: 'Because your container is currently full-width (long lines), you should actually go even higher: 1.7 until you fix the container width. Once you constrain the line length with a max-width, you can dial back to 1.5–1.6'

expectations.text: Gives specific numeric recommendations (e.g., max-width: 65ch, line-height: 1.5)
expectations.evidence: CSS block: `p { max-width: 65ch; line-height: 1.6; }` provided explicitly

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are tight and working well. The proportional relationship assertion is a strong discriminator.
